perturbation.original_text: 1. Definitions. As used herein, the following terms shall be defined as set forth below: a. "Contract Period" shall mean that period of time from February 21, 2011 through December 31, 2012.
perturbation.changed_text: 1. Definitions. As used herein, the following terms shall be defined as set forth below: a. "Contract Period" shall mean that period of time from February 21, 2011 through December 31, 2011.
perturbation.explanation: Modifying the definition of "Contract Period" to end in 2011 creates an internal inconsistency because other sections still reference obligations extending into 2012. This change makes the contract ambiguous about the term's duration.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Term of this Agreement shall be for the Contract Period unless it is terminated or extended as set forth in this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the 'Contract Period' definition to end in 2011, while the contradicted text states that the agreement's term shall be for the 'Contract Period' unless terminated or extended. Since the changed 'Contract Period' now ends in 2011, it creates a contradiction because any obligations or clauses relying on the original 'Contract Period' extending into 2012 are now inconsistent and unenforceable, as the defined term no longer supports that duration. This aligns with the definition of Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction because redefining the 'Contract Period' causes conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: g. Supply of Endorsed Products. Company shall deliver to North, at no charge, and in a timely fashion, a reasonable number of Endorsed Products for North's personal use each Contract Year during the Contract Period. All postage expenses associated with the delivery of the Endorsed Products shall be borne by Company.
perturbation.changed_text: g. Supply of Products. Company shall deliver to North, at no charge, and in a timely fashion, a reasonable number of products for North's personal use each Contract Year during the Term. All postage expenses associated with the delivery of the products shall be borne by Company.
perturbation.explanation: By changing "Endorsed Products" to "products" and "Contract Period" to "Term," the company becomes obligated to provide North with a potentially unlimited amount of various products rather than the originally stipulated endorsed goods, altering the scope of their responsibility.
perturbation.contradicted_text: As used herein, the following terms shall be defined as set forth below: a. "Contract Period" shall mean that period of time from February 21, 2011 through December 31, 2011. b. "Contract Year" shall mean the specific period of time during the Contract Period as more specifically set forth below: · Contract Year 2011 (2/21/11 - 12/31/11) · Contract Year 2012 (1/1/12- 12/31/12) c. "Contract Territory" shall mean the world. d. "North's Likeness" shall mean and include North's name, image, photograph, voice, initials, signature, biographical information, and persona. f. "North's Endorsement" shall mean and include North's public statements and comments about Company's Product. Page 3 of 12 Source: PERFORMANCE SPORTS BRANDS, INC., S-1, 9/9/2011 g. "Endorsed Product(s)" shall only mean and include Company's irons, woods, wedges and putters.
perturbation.justification: The changed text broadens the scope of 'Endorsed Products' to simply 'products'. The contradicted text specifically defines 'Endorsed Product(s)' as only including Company's irons, woods, wedges and putters. This creates a direct contradiction because the changed text implies that North could receive any product from the Company, whereas the original contract limited it to specific endorsed products. Also, changing 'Contract Period' to 'Term' creates ambiguity because 'Term' is not defined in the contradicted text, potentially leading to disputes over the contract's duration, especially since 'Contract Period' and 'Contract Year' *are* specifically defined.

perturbation.original_text: 29. Force Majeure. In the event any of the appearances set forth in this Agreement are cancelled or delayed due to matters of "Force Majeure", as such term is defined below, Company and North shall use their reasonable efforts to reschedule such appearance as soon as possible following such cancellation or delay. As used herein, the term "Force Majeure" shall mean war, civil unrest, acts of God, unusually severe weather, environmental matters, failure of common carrier, governmental action, labor disputes, casualty, illness, injury and/or emergency.
perturbation.changed_text: 29. Unforeseen Circumstances. In the event any of the appearances set forth in this Agreement are cancelled or delayed due to matters of "Unforeseen Circumstances", as such term is defined below, Company and North shall use their reasonable efforts to reschedule such appearance as soon as possible following such cancellation or delay. As used herein, the term "Unforeseen Circumstances" shall mean war, civil unrest, acts of God, unusually severe weather, environmental matters, failure of common carrier, governmental action, labor disputes, casualty, illness, injury and/or emergency. However, North's prior commitments shall also be considered Unforeseen Circumstances.
perturbation.explanation: Modifying the term "Force Majeure" to "Unforeseen Circumstances" and adding "North's prior commitments" to the definition creates a conflict. "Force Majeure" typically refers to external events, while including North's commitments broadens the definition, potentially excusing North from obligations for reasons not traditionally covered. The contract now has internal conflict on what is an excusable absence.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Appearances. a. Production Appearances. i. Contract Year 2011. North agrees to make one (1) Production Appearance on behalf of Company during Contract Year 2011 for the purpose of producing the following: (1) one 30-minute infomercial; (2) one 30-second television commercial; (3) one demonstration dvd on how to use the Endorsed Product. (4) product testimonials and (5) still photographs for print advertisements and packaging (hereinafter referred to as "Production Appearance"). Said Production Appearance shall take place on February 22 & 23, 2011 in or around Phoenix, AZ. The Production Appearance shall be approximately nine (9) hours in duration on February 22nd and five (5) hours in duration on February 23rd. During such Production Appearance, North shall be permitted to wear the apparel of his choice. ii. Contract Year 2012. North agrees to make one (1) Production Appearance on behalf of Company during Contract Year 2012 for the purpose of producing either new or updated Advertising Materials. Said Production Appearance shall take place at a mutually agreed upon time and location. The Production Appearance shall be approximately nine (9) hours in duration on the first day and five (5) hours in duration on the second day. During such Production Appearance, North shall be permitted to wear the apparel of his choice. Page 4 of 12 Source: PERFORMANCE SPORTS BRANDS, INC., S-1, 9/9/2011 b. Additional Production/Promotional Appearances. If requested by Company, North agrees to use reasonable effort to make Additional Production/Promotional Appearances on behalf of Company (hereinafter referred to as "Additional Production/Promotional Appearances"). Should Company request for North to conduct Additional Production/Promotional Appearances and North agrees to appear, Company shall pay North an additional fee for such appearance(s) as set forth in paragraph 6.d. below. North has no obligation to make such appearance. c. Voice-Over Appearances. If requested by Company, North shall make one (1) voice over appearance during the Term. The voice over appearance shall take place at North's personal residence or at another mutually agreed upon location in Wisconsin, and shall not exceed two (2) hours in duration. d. Promotional Appearance. If requested by Company, North agrees to make one (1) promotional appearance each Contract Year during the Term at certain events to promote the Endorsed Products. Said appearance shall be approximately three (3) hours in duration. e. Promotion/Media Activities. North will use reasonable efforts to mention the benefits of Company's Endorsed Products when North is being interviewed or North is participating in Public Events as defined below. f. Expenses. If travel is necessary to provide the services set forth in this Agreement, Company shall provide or reimburse all such travel expenses of North, including round-trip first-class airfare on the airline of North's choice, first-class hotel accommodations, reasonable meal and ground transportation expenses. Company shall deliver the airline tickets to North at least seven (7) days prior to the scheduled appearance, and reimburse North for other such expenses within ten (10) days after receipt of the expense receipts. g. Scheduling. All appearances shall be scheduled on dates, times and locations that are mutually agreed upon by Company and North. All such appearances shall be scheduled through North's duly authorized agent for this project-Fidelity Sports Group, LLC.
perturbation.justification: The original text defined 'Force Majeure' as external events excusing performance. The changed text redefines it as 'Unforeseen Circumstances' and includes 'North's prior commitments.' This directly contradicts the 'Appearances' section by allowing North to potentially avoid agreed-upon appearances due to his own scheduling conflicts, which is not a traditionally accepted reason for excusing performance under a force majeure clause. This creates a direct conflict regarding North's obligations, leading to uncertainty in enforcement. The change breaks compatibility between the 'Unforeseen Circumstances' definition and the obligation to fulfill 'Appearances' as scheduled, rendering the obligation unclear.
